message:
feat(core,abi): port pwd/grp/shadow line serializers to core (bd-pgs-epic)

Lifts the format!()-with-from_utf8_unchecked line serialization out of
putpwent (glibc_internal_abi.rs:5685, ~40 lines), putgrent
(glibc_internal_abi.rs:5619, ~35 lines), and putspent
(unistd_abi.rs:13366, ~37 lines) into pure-safe core::{pwd, grp,
pwd::shadow} formatters operating on byte slices.

Public surface (all in #![deny(unsafe_code)] core):
  pwd::format_passwd_line(name, passwd, uid, gid, gecos, dir, shell,
                          out: &mut Vec<u8>)
  grp::format_group_line(name, passwd, gid, members: &[&[u8]],
                         out: &mut Vec<u8>)
  pwd::shadow::format_shadow_line(name, passwd, lstchg, min, max,
                                  warn, inact, expire, flag,
                                  out: &mut Vec<u8>)

Each writes one canonical colon-separated record terminated with `\n`,
allocates nothing beyond the caller-supplied Vec, and round-trips with
the existing parse_passwd_line / parse_group_line.

Notable correctness improvement: format_shadow_line correctly emits
empty strings for the glibc -1 numeric sentinel and the u64::MAX flag
sentinel — matching real putspent. The previous abi impl wrote the
literal "-1", which would not round-trip through parse_shadow_line on
fields like inact/expire that are normally absent.

The C-ABI marshalling — *const libc::passwd/group/spwd field reads
via CStr with NULL fallbacks, fwrite to FILE* — stays at the boundary.

Verified:
  - 17 new core unit tests pass (5 pwd, 7 grp, 7 shadow)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests still pass

Closes bd-pgs-epic.
